enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: The inn is warm. Wood-smoke, day-old bread, a fire the color of regret. A slim man at the counter sets his ledger down as you approach, and his smile is polite enough to be nothing at all.

hello.speaker: tomas
hello.text: Welcome to the Whispering Hollow. The name is older than the inn, in case anyone has told you otherwise. What will it be — a bed, a meal, or just the fire?

choices.text: I have the moonpetals you asked for.
About the room you keep — there is a guest who is not coming back.
I found his pack in Mossbarrow. The journal is here.
[The room you keep aired. For me, now?]
What's the town been like lately?
I'm looking for a scholar. An Elven man, Veyrin.

herb-offer.speaker: tomas
herb-offer.text: The healer left town two months ago. I have been making do with what poultices I can manage, but I am running low on moonpetals. They grow in the woods and the depths — pale things, purple-edged. Three would last me through the season.

herb-accept.speaker: tomas
herb-accept.text: I will pay what I can. It is not much, but it is more than the petals would fetch on the road. Be careful where you pick them — they grow in places that are quiet for a reason.

herb-turnin.speaker: tomas
herb-turnin.text: Three moonpetals. Still fresh. You have done me a kindness, and I do not forget kindnesses. They are too rare in this town to waste.

herb-done.speaker: tomas
herb-done.text: Thirty-five gold, and my thanks. The fire and a bed are yours whenever you need them. No charge, for tonight.

bed.speaker: tomas
bed.text: Ten gold for a night. Twelve if you want the east room — it has the window that does not stick. A hot meal is included either way.

regulars.speaker: tomas
regulars.text: Fewer, these past months. Mostly adventurers now. The merchants stopped coming when the Greenhollow road went quiet. Some of the rooms are still held for those who paid ahead and have not come back to sleep in them.

veyrin.speaker: tomas
veyrin.text: Veyrin. Quiet man. Paid in moonlight silver, once, which I took because he asked kindly. Walked out one morning toward the woods and said he would be back by supper. His bed is still made. I keep his room for him. Call it a habit.

veyrin-offer.speaker: tomas
veyrin-offer.text: If you find him — or find what is left of him — I would like to know. I keep the room either way. I would just like to stop setting out the tea.

about-tomas.speaker: tomas
about-tomas.text: Innkeeper. Before that, a courier on the Ashenmere road. Before that, young and careless, which is almost the same thing. I inherited this place from a woman who said I owed her something. She was right. I have been paying it off in firewood and warm soup ever since.

about-tomas-2.speaker: tomas
about-tomas-2.text: I do not miss the road. I miss the people you met on it. There is a difference.

town-lately.speaker: tomas
town-lately.text: Quiet, and not the peaceful kind. The market days stopped when the last merchant decided the coin was not worth the risk. Children who used to play past the well stay closer to their doors now. Even the dogs are nervous.

town-lately-2.speaker: tomas
town-lately-2.text: That is partly why I keep the fire going through the night. Some people just need a lit window to walk toward.

tomas-rumor.speaker: tomas
tomas-rumor.text: A pair of hunters came back from the Greenhollow road last week without their horses. Claimed something large had crossed the path just before dawn — too big for a wolf, wrong shape for a bear. They could not agree on what it was, but they agreed on one thing.

tomas-rumor-2.speaker: tomas
tomas-rumor-2.text: They drank until they stopped shaking, paid their bill, and left at midday. Neither of them came back through. I keep a room aired, just in case.

leave.speaker: tomas
leave.text: As you like. The fire will be here. So will I.

hire-tomas.speaker: tomas
hire-tomas.text: Sixty gold. I have some field medicine — not much, but enough to keep a wound from ending you between blows. I have done it before, in harder times than these.

hire-tomas-confirm.speaker: tomas
hire-tomas-confirm.text: I will leave the inn to Marta. She manages better than she lets on. Let us go before I reconsider.

tomas-regular.speaker: tomas
tomas-regular.text: ...You noticed. I did not air it for you. Not at first. But the bed has been warm for three weeks running, and I have stopped writing the old name in the ledger when I make it up. Take it as you like.

tomas-regular-2.speaker: tomas
tomas-regular-2.text: The east room. Window that does not stick. No charge tonight. Pay me back by walking out the door tomorrow morning, and walking back in by nightfall. That is all I ask of regulars.

empty-room-offer.speaker: tomas
empty-room-offer.text: ...You read me well. The east room. Pre-paid through the season. The man slept three nights in it before he left for Mossbarrow with a notebook and a fresh shave. That was thirty-eight days ago. The bed is still made.

empty-room-offer-2.speaker: tomas
empty-room-offer-2.text: I cannot let the room rot. I cannot let the next traveler sleep on a stranger's pillow. Find his pack in those ruins, if it is still where he dropped it. Bring me what is in it. After that, I can fold the bedding without lying to myself.

empty-room-accept.speaker: tomas
empty-room-accept.text: He kept his belongings tidy. The pack will not be hard to spot for someone who is looking. The hard part will be reading what he wrote in it.

empty-room-tomas-decision.speaker: narrator
empty-room-tomas-decision.text: You set the pack on the counter. The leather is salt-streaked, the strap chewed at one end by something with patience. Tomas does not open it. He looks at the journal you have set beside it — the one you read by lantern in the ruins, the one whose last page is a poem.

empty-room-tomas-decision-2.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-decision-2.text: A poem. To Vira. Of course it is to Vira. He paid in moonlight silver, and now I know why. The merchants travel together, and the ones who do not come home travel furthest. I should have guessed.

empty-room-tomas-decision-3.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-decision-3.text: What you do with that book is not for me to say. I have his pack and that is what I asked you to bring. The page... is between you and her.

choices.text: I'll give the journal to Vira.
Take it. The dead are yours to bury here.

empty-room-tomas-defer.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-defer.text: Thank you. The pack stays with me. The journal goes to her. That is the right shape of it. Three hundred and twenty gold for the work, and a meal whenever the road brings you back. It is the most I can give without taking from someone else.

empty-room-tomas-defer-close.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-defer-close.text: Walk softly to her shop. She will know what you are bringing before you put it on the counter. She always does.

empty-room-tomas-keep.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-keep.text: Then I will read the page tonight. And tomorrow I will fold the bedding, and the room will be airing for the next stranger by the week's end. That is how this trade closes its accounts.

empty-room-tomas-keep-2.speaker: tomas
empty-room-tomas-keep-2.text: Three hundred and twenty gold. Three hundred and eighty experience. The room pays its own debts, in time. Thank you. Truly.
